focus.description: Describe the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing three-dimensional figures, as in plane sections of right rectangular prisms and right rectangular pyramids.

descendants.description: Identify the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of three-dimensional objects, and identify three-dimensional objects generated by rotations of two-dimensional objects.
(+) Give an informal argument using Cavalieri's principle for the formulas for the volume of a sphere and other solid figures.

learningComponents.description: Describe and identify the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing a right rectangular prism or pyramid by a plane perpendicular to one of the faces
learningComponents.author: Achievement Network

learningComponents.description: Describe and identify the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing a right rectangular prism or pyramid by a plane perpendicular to the base or a  plane parallel to the base
learningComponents.author: Achievement Network

learningComponents.description: Describe and identify the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing a right rectangular prism or pyramid by a plane that is not parallel or perpendicular to a base
learningComponents.author: Achievement Network

learningComponents.description: Describe and identify the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing a three-dimensional figure by a plane perpendicular to one of the faces
learningComponents.author: Achievement Network

learningComponents.description: Describe and identify the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing a three-dimensional figure by a plane perpendicular to the base or a  plane parallel to the base
learningComponents.author: Achievement Network
